full_text:
FACS Staining Protocol_drug treatment

Antibody Panel

All mentioned antibodies and reagents are in Kekäläinen / Arstila lab / TRIMM: +4°C called “Snowgie”, -20°C called “Bruni”. *CD56 FITC and ki67 PE-CY7 are in a box called Anh’s project (in Snowgie)

Bring with to Haartman from BM:

1 mL, 200 uL and 10 uL pipet and corresponding tips

1.5 mL eppendorfs

50 mL falcon for waste

Surface Staining Protocol

Aliquot up to 3 x 106 cells into an Eppendorf tube, add 900 µL FACS staining buffer (2% FBS, 2mM EDTA in PBS; aliquot in Färkkilä +4C cell culture) and centrifuge (6 min, +4°C, 400g).

NOTE! During the staining protocol a significant number of cells are lost,

so in the beginning 3-4 x 106 can be collected.

Meanwhile, prepare the antibody master mix for the surface antigens:

For each sample you should prepare 50 µL of antibody master mix

This mix contains all the antibodies in the panel at a specific titration to maximize the distance between the positive and negative populations.

For surface staining, as the buffer use either FACS staining buffer OR when multiple BV (BrillianViolet antibodies are used, as in here) use Brilliant Stain buffer (BD Horizon #563794, Kekäläinen +4°C). Add the remaining volume (to make up to 50 µL) of FACS staining buffer – it is a good idea to add this to an Eppendorf before adding the master mix.

NOTE! Dead cell marker (DCM) is not an antibody and can be detrimental to other antibodies in the master mix if stored together for too long. Thus, add the DCM to the antibody master mix just before adding the mix to the cell pellet.

After the centrifugation, discard the supernatant by pipetting, leaving the cell pellet intact. Resuspend the pellet into a 48 µL antibody master mix by gently pipetting up and down. Incubate for 30 min, +4°C in dark.

Wash: after incubation, add 900 µL FACS staining buffer and centrifuge (6min, +4°C, 400g). Discard the supernatant and proceed to the intracellular staining.

(Optional: if not running the sample on the same day, fix cells after the wash as follows:

Resuspend the pellet with 500 uL 2% PFA in 1x PBS and incubate for 10 min, +4°C, protected from

light.

Add 1 mL of 1x PBS and centrifuge (6min, +4°C, 400g). Discard the supernatant.

Repeat the wash with 1 mL 1x PBS, centrifuge as before and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the pellet in 900 uL FACS staining buffer and store the sample at +4°C, protected from

light (tube wrapped in tin foil) and proceed with the intracellular staining within 3 weeks (max!).)

Intracellular Staining

Prepare the permeabilization solution, 500 µL / tube, by diluting the permeabilization concentrate 1:4 (125 µL : 375 µL per sample). Reagents: Invitrogen Fixation / Permeabilization concentrate (#00-5123-43) and eBioscience Fixation / Perm Diluent (#00-5223-56), both in Kekäläinen +4°C.

From the previous STEP 5, discard the supernatant and resuspend with permeabilization solution (500 µL / tube). Incubate for 45 min, +4°C in dark. Meanwhile, prepare the intracellular antibody master mix. Note! As the buffer use permeabilization buffer (1 X; in a 50 ml falcon tube on the door of Kekäläinen +4°C).

Wash the cells by adding 1 mL of permeabilization buffer (1 X; same as above) and centrifuging (6 min +4°C, 400g). Discard the supernatant.

After the last centrifugation, discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet into 48 µL antibody master mix. Incubate for 30 min, +4°C in dark.

Wash with 1 mL of 1 X permeabilization buffer. Centrifuge (6min, +4°C, 400g) and discard the supernatant.

Resuspend the pellet into 250 µL FACS staining buffer and pipet the sample through Falcon 5 ml polystyrene tube with cell-strainer cap (#352235, Kekäläinen / Arstila drawer. Protip: if all the tubes are finished in the drawer, check the cupboard next to the sink “cyan tubes”)

Export the data to a hard drive.

Analyse the samples using FlowJo.

Protocol for Single Colour Compensations

Single cell compensations should be run when using or setting up a new panel -> and then around every month if you continue to use the panel for subsequent samples.

Use Falcon 5 ml polystyrene round-bottom tubes (#352052, Kekäläinen / Arstila drawer)

Label each tube with corresponding fluorochrome AND one negative.

For each tube add 1 drop of BD CompBeads Negative Control (#51-90-9001291; Kekäläinen +4°C door, top shelf) AND 1 drop of BD CompBeads Anti-Mouse Ig (#51-90-9001229) or Anti-Rat / Anti-Hamster Ig (#51-90-9000949) depending on the antibody-fluorochrome tested.

Add 1 µL of a single fluorochrome conjugated antibody to the corresponding labelled FACS tube. Note! If using DCM, use any other antibody-conjugate with the same fluorochrome. Note 2.0! Don´t add any fluorochrome to the negative control -> incubate 30 mins, +4°C in dark, and add 250 µL of FACS Staining buffer before running.

When Setting up a panel for the first time, these compensations should be used to determine the PMT voltages for each fluorochrome
